title: chewy citrusy british cherry fry
description: a delicious, chewy dessert featuring cherries, inspired by the simplicity of traditional British cuisine. perfect for a Buddhist temple-friendly gathering.
instructions: mix together the flour, sugar, and a pinch of salt in a large bowl.
add the softened butter and mix until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
add the orange juice, lemon zest, and vanilla extract, and mix until a dough forms.
beat in the eggs and knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth.
divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
flatten each ball into a disk shape and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
dollop a spoonful of cherry jam in the center of each disk and fold the dough over to enclose the filling.
bake for 18-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
